title: Fix(graph): Allow any Line subclass as edge_type in Graph/DiGraph
body:
Overview
- This change broadens edge rendering in Graph and DiGraph to support any Line subclass as edge_type, not just the default Line. This enables using specialized edge types (e.g., LabeledLine) across graphs.

Motivation
- Previously, Graph and DiGraph assumed edge_type would align with the first two arguments (start, end) in a Line-based edge. Subclasses of Line (like LabeledLine) may require different edge construction semantics or additional configuration. This PR makes edge_type extensible to arbitrary Line subclasses, improving flexibility and extensibility.

What changes here
- Core: Graph and DiGraph now accept and propagate an edge_type (a Line subclass) when creating edges. The default remains Line to preserve existing behavior.
- Edge creation paths updated: _populate_edge_dict and _add_edge now instantiate edge Mobjects using self.edge_type (or a similar attribute) instead of hard-coding Line. For DiGraph, per-edge tip configurations remain supported via existing structures, and are applied to the chosen edge_type.
- Testing: Tests in test_graph.py updated to include scenarios using LabeledLine as edge_type, ensuring both Graph and DiGraph support this usage.

Backward compatibility
- If no edge_type is provided, behavior is unchanged (Line is used by default).

Impact and risk
- Low risk to existing users due to default preserving current behavior.
- Potential edge-case considerations include: per-edge updates for edge subclasses (e.g., LabeledLine may expose additional attributes). The update methods should ensure endpoint updates also reposition any extra per-edge elements (like labels) if applicable.

Follow-ups (concise)
- Consider adding explicit docstrings for the new edge_type parameter (PARAMETERS section) where applicable.
- Extend tests to cover additional edge subclasses beyond LabeledLine for broader validation.

step_plan.summary: This PR introduces a configurable edge_type for Graph and DiGraph, enabling any Line subclass to be used for edges while preserving backward compatibility with the default Line. It propagates edge_type through edge creation and updates, and requires corresponding tests to validate LabeledLine and other Line subclasses in both Graph and DiGraph.

steps.operation: Add edge_type parameter to GenericGraph.__init__ and store as an instance attribute
steps.reason: Introduce a configurable edge_type (defaulting to Line) so Graph/DiGraph can render edges with any Line subclass while preserving backward compatibility.
steps.side_effects: Requires updating all callers that construct GenericGraph and tests to either pass a specific edge_type or rely on the default. Potentially affects object serialization/deserialization if edge_type is part of state.

steps.operation: Update GenericGraph._add_edge to instantiate edges using self.edge_type rather than a hard-coded Line
steps.reason: Ensure per-edge construction uses the configured edge_type so diverse Line subclasses can be rendered (e.g., LabeledLine) with proper edge_config application.
steps.side_effects: Edges created for Graph may differ in subclass behavior; ensure compatibility with edge_config and potential additional attributes on edge subclasses.

steps.operation: Modify GenericGraph._populate_edge_dict to create edges with self.edge_type for undirected graphs
steps.reason: Propagate the configurable edge_type when building edges from the underlying graph data, enabling using non-Line edge classes across Graph variants.
steps.side_effects: If edge creation relies on specific Line subclass APIs, ensure edge_type subclasses are compatible with existing edge_config structures.

steps.operation: Extend Graph._populate_edge_dict to utilize the configured edge_type when creating undirected edges
steps.reason: Apply the same edge_type customization to Graph (undirected) paths to maintain consistency with GenericGraph behavior.
steps.side_effects: May require coordination with DiGraph paths to avoid duplication of edge construction logic.

steps.operation: Update Graph.update_edges to reposition edges for any Line subclass used as edge_type
steps.reason: Ensure dynamic updates (e.g., vertex movement) correctly reposition edges regardless of concrete edge subclass.
steps.side_effects: Edge subclass internals (e.g., labels or extra visuals) must update in concert with vertex movements; may require additional hooks if subclasses differ significantly.

steps.operation: Add edge_type parameter to DiGraph.__init__ and propagate to base GenericGraph behavior
steps.reason: Support symmetric propagation of edge_type in directed graphs, keeping default behavior unchanged when edge_type is not provided.
steps.side_effects: Code paths that instantiate DiGraph must pass edge_type through; ensures backward compatibility by defaulting to Line.

steps.operation: Update DiGraph._populate_edge_dict to instantiate directed edges using self.edge_type and apply per-edge tip_config
steps.reason: Allow any Line subclass to render directed edges with appropriate tip configuration while preserving existing per-edge configuration flows.
steps.side_effects: Edge subclass may require additional initialization parameters for tips; tests may need to cover LabeledLine with tip_config.

steps.operation: Modify DiGraph._add_edge to create directed edges via self.edge_type and apply per-edge tip_config
steps.reason: Ensure all edge creation for DiGraph respects the configured edge_type and associated per-edge configurations.
steps.side_effects: Possible API surface changes for edge creation; ensure consistency with Graph._add_edge and keep backward compatibility via default edge_type.

steps.operation: Update DiGraph.update_edges to reposition edges for any Line subclass used as edge_type
steps.reason: Maintain correct rendering when vertices move across directed graphs for any edge_type subclass.
steps.side_effects: If edge_type subclasses expose unique repositioning needs, ensure these are accommodated (e.g., dynamic label positions).
